request.description:
Helper starts the assigned job.
Status: ASSIGNED → IN_PROGRESS

Helper marks the job as done. Customer receives notification to review.
Status: IN_PROGRESS → WAITING_FOR_APPROVAL

Customer approves the completed work.
Status: WAITING_FOR_APPROVAL → COMPLETED

Triggers escrow release automatically:
- Payment.status = RELEASED
- Wallet.pending_balance -= helper_amount
- Wallet.available_balance += helper_amount
- WalletTransaction type=JOB_EARNING created

Socket.io event

Event: send_offer
Payload: { negotiationId: string, amount: number }

Broadcast: offer_received
{ offer: { id, sender_id, sender, amount, created_at } }

Example flow:
  Job budget = $30
  Helper sends_offer $50
  Customer sends_offer $40
  Helper sends_offer $45
  Customer accept_offer -> locked at $45

Socket.io event

Event: reject_negotiation
Payload: { negotiationId: string }

Customer ends -> status = REJECTED
Helper ends -> status = CANCELLED

Broadcast: negotiation_rejected
{ negotiation: { id, status }, rejected_by: userId }

Creates a Stripe PaymentIntent for the selected job.

Returns:
- clientSecret: use with Stripe.js SDK on the frontend to collect card details
- amount: total customer pays
- platformFee: commission deducted immediately
- helperAmount: held in escrow until job approved

Price used:
- is_negotiable = false → job.budget
- is_negotiable = true  → Negotiation.final_amount (must be ACCEPTED)

Get payment details for a job (accessible by both customer and helper).

Shows: amount, platformFee, helperAmount, status, stripe_payment_intent

This endpoint is called by Stripe — NOT by the frontend.

Handled events:
- payment_intent.succeeded → Funds escrow, assigns job, rejects other apps, opens conversation, credits helper pending_balance
- payment_intent.payment_failed → Marks payment FAILED

Requires: stripe-signature header (set by Stripe automatically)
Do NOT call this manually — test using Stripe CLI: stripe listen --forward-to localhost:5000/api/v1/payments/webhook

Returns wallet balances and Stripe Connect status:
- available_balance: ready to withdraw
- pending_balance: held in escrow
- stripe_onboarding_done: true if Stripe account is connected and ready

Creates a Stripe Connect Express account for the helper.

Returns:
- accountId: the Stripe Connect account ID
- onboardingUrl: redirect the helper to this URL in the browser

The helper completes their Stripe onboarding (adds debit card or bank account).
Stripe then redirects back to FRONTEND_URL/wallet/connect/success

Call this endpoint after the helper returns from Stripe onboarding.
Checks with Stripe whether onboarding is complete and updates wallet.stripe_onboarding_done.

Returns:
- onboardingComplete: boolean
- chargesEnabled: boolean
- payoutsEnabled: boolean

Paginated transaction history.
type filter: JOB_EARNING | COMMISSION | WITHDRAWAL | REFUND

Helper's withdrawal history.
status filter: PENDING | PROCESSING | COMPLETED | FAILED | REJECTED

Request a Stripe Transfer to the helper's connected account.

Pre-requisite: helper must have completed Stripe Connect onboarding.

Flow:
1. Validates amount <= available_balance
2. Creates stripe.transfers.create() → money moves to helper's Stripe account
3. Deducts from available_balance
4. Creates Withdrawal record (status=PENDING)
5. Creates WITHDRAWAL wallet transaction

Stripe then pays out to the helper's linked debit card or bank account per their payout schedule (instant or next business day).

On REJECTED/FAILED: amount is refunded back to available_balance automatically.
Admin only: All withdrawal requests with helper details and Stripe transfer ID stored in bank_details JSON.

Admin manually updates status (e.g., after verifying Stripe transfer).

REJECTED or FAILED → automatically refunds available_balance + creates REFUND transaction.

Submit a review for a completed job.

Only the customer of the job can leave a review.
Recalculates helper average rating and total reviews, and sets Job status to CLOSED.
Get all reviews received by the authenticated helper.
Get reviews received by a helper (publicly accessible).

Returns aggregated analytics and stats for the admin panel:
- Users breakdown (active, suspended, helpers)
- Jobs breakdown (open, completed, disputed, etc.)
- Financials (volume, escrow held, platform fees)
- Recent activities (new jobs, payments, verification requests, withdrawals)
Returns monthly statistics for the last 6 months (revenue, volume, total jobs, completed jobs) formatted for frontend data charts.
List all payments on the platform with search (customer, helper, job title) and status filters.
List helper wallets showing available/pending balances.
